etymology.description: semantic 白(白) (bái, "white") + phonetic 勺(勺) (sháo) -> possessive/attributive particle
etymology.interpretation: The character 的 originally combined 白(白) (bái, "white") as the semantic element, denoting brightness or clarity, with 勺(勺) (sháo), providing the phonetic component for sound. Over time, the meaning shifted from this literal sense to function as a grammatical particle marking possession or attribution, likely due to a sound borrowing or figurative extension from the clarity or specification inherent in 'white.'

examples.chinese: 这是我的书(這是我的書)。
他的新车很漂亮(他的新車很漂亮)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xīn chē hěn piàoliang.
examples.english: His new car is very beautiful.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢听大海的声音(我喜歡聽大海的聲音)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān tīng dàhǎi de shēngyīn.
examples.english: I like listening to the sound of the sea.
